data.title: Goodman Price Target Cut 8.1% to A$39.50/Share by Jarden
PubMatic's Chief Accounting Officer Sells Shares to Cover Tax Obligations
data.description:
Reporter NameGimbel Lisa
RelationshipCHIEF ACCOUNTING OFFICER
TypeSell
Amount$14,018
SEC FilingForm 4

Lisa Gimbel, the Chief Accounting Officer of PubMatic, sold 911 shares of Class A Common Stock on February 18, 2025, at a weighted average price of $15.3884 per share, totaling $14,018. The sale was conducted to cover tax withholding obligations related to the vesting of restricted stock units. Following the transaction, Gimbel directly owns 4,791 shares of PubMatic's Class A Common Stock.

SEC Filing: PubMatic, Inc. [ PUBM ] - Form 4 - Feb. 19, 2025

data.title: CRISPR Therapeutics Executives Sell Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Kulkarni SamarthChief Executive OfficerSell$1,006,580Form 4
Kasinger James R.General Counsel and SecretarySell$619,492Form 4
Bruno JulianneChief Operating OfficerSell$17,582Form 4

CRISPR Therapeutics AG has seen a series of share sales by its top executives, according to recent SEC Form 4 filings. Chief Executive Officer Samarth Kulkarni sold 18,568 Common Shares on February 18 and 19, 2025, for a total of $1,006,580, at weighted average prices of $55.05 and $52.8. Following these transactions, Kulkarni directly owns 189,973 Common Shares and indirectly owns 85,622 Common Shares through The Kulkarni 2023 GRAT.

James R. Kasinger, the company's General Counsel and Secretary, sold 11,542 Common Shares on February 18 and 19, 2025, for a total of $619,492, at prices of $53.85 and $52.8. Post-sale, Kasinger directly owns 74,880 Common Shares.

Chief Operating Officer Bruno Julianne sold 333 common shares on February 19, 2025, at a price of $52.8 per share, totaling $17,582. After the sale, Julianne directly owns 7,062 common shares and indirectly owns 7,088 shares through The Julianne Bruno 2022 GRAT.

data.title: Triple Flag Precious Metals Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.055 a Share, Payable March 14 to Holders of Record March 3
Openlane Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Openlane Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 21 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 16 cents. The mean expectation of seven analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 21 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 19 cents to 22 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 16.3% to $455.00 million from a year ago; analysts expected $420.30 million.

  • Openlane Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 29 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $52.3 million.

  • Openlane Inc shares had risen by 1.4% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 1.6% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 4 "strong buy" or "buy," 4 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the auto vehicles, parts & service retailers peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Openlane Inc is $22.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data February 19 at 10:39 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Soccer-Slot unhappy as Liverpool miss chance to go 10 points clear
data.description:
Liverpool manager Arne Slot would have signed up for being eight points clear at the top of the Premier League with 12 games left of his first season in charge but he could not hide his frustration after a 2-2 draw at Aston Villa on Wednesday.

Victory would have sent his team 10 points clear of Arsenal having played one game more but his side dropped points for the second time in three games to leave the door slightly ajar.

"Not so happy to be honest. I think if you look back at the game, we created far more chances then them," Slot told TNT Sports. "I think we did everything we had to do to get a result over here, maybe even a win."

It was a night of mixed emotions for Liverpool who dominated early on and took the lead in the 28th minute thanks to Mohamed Salah's 24th league goal of the season.

Goals by Youri Tielemans and Ollie Watkins gave Villa a halftime lead before Trent Alexander-Arnold's deflected shot just past the hour levelled it up.

Liverpool substitute Darwin Nunez missed a sitter and Slot's side had their hearts in their mouths at the death as Villa substitute Donyell Malen shaved the post with a shot.

Slot's side had 17 goal attempts to Villa's nine but were left to rue a lack of precision in front of goal.

"In the final minute (Villa) could have won it," Slot, whose side have lost just one league game all season, said.

"But if you look at all our chances in a row it's clear that I'm not happy with the 2-2. To come here it's difficult one because of the team they have, because of the support they have from the fans, but I think we were close to playing a very, very, very good game."

Second-placed Arsenal now have a game in hand and will get the chance to ratchet up the pressure on Liverpool at the weekend. Arsenal host West Ham United on Saturday and a win would cut the gap to five points ahead of Liverpool's trip to champions Manchester City on Sunday.

"There's always pressure if you play for Liverpool, that's the same if you play for Arsenal," Slot said. "We want to achieve something together this season, and we have all the ingredients for it. Still 12 games to go and the Champions League, but we're happy where we are.

"I think we've played a few too many games where we deserve more than we got. I don't mean the Everton game when I look at the quality of play, but it was, of course, a big disappointment to concede in the last minute."

One concern for Slot was an injury to Conor Bradley who had come off the bench to replace Alexander-Arnold.

"It happened in the sprint," he said. "You never know what it is, but if a player goes out it's never a good sign."

data.title: Karman Holdings Insider Sold Shares Worth $1,599,994, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Stephanie Sawhill, Chief Growth Officer, on February 14, 2025, sold 72,727 shares in Karman Holdings for $1,599,994. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Sawhill has control over a total of 628,939 common shares of the company, with 628,939 shares held directly.

data.description: Feb 19 (Reuters) - Sienna Senior Living Q4 adjusted revenue CAD 246.3 million.

data.title: Axis Capital Board Authorizes $400 Million Share Repurchase Program; Maintains Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
Axis Capital Holdings late Wednesday announced a $400 million share repurchase program.

The program replaces the company's prior $300 million share repurchase authorization.

Meanwhile, the company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.44 per share, payable April 17 to shareholders of record on March 31.

data.title: Sienna Senior Living Brief: AFFO per share increased by 25.1% in Q4 2024 to $0.304
China Mining International's Unit Faces Court-Ordered Asset Seizure
data.description:
China Mining International said its 63.1%-owned subsidiary, Henan Zhongnong Huasheng Agricultural Technology Co.,

has informed the company of a court-ordered seizure of fixed assets belonging to its wholly owned unit, Henan Zhongnong Huasheng Industrial, according to a Wednesday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The seized assets, valued at more than 15 million yuan, include a sorting workshop, cold storage facilities, and fruit and vegetable sorting lines.

data.title: Amplitude Q4 Adjusted Earnings Fall, Revenue Rises; Sets 2025 Guidance
data.description:
Amplitude reported Q4 adjusted earnings late Wednesday of $0.02 per diluted share, down from $0.04 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.01.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was $78.1 million, up from $71.4 million a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $76.7 million.

The company said it expects a Q1 non-GAAP loss of $0.01 to $0.03. Four analysts polled by FactSet expect non-GAAP EPS of $0.01. The company expects revenue for the quarter of $78.5 million to $80.5 million. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect $77.7 million.

For 2025, the company expects non-GAAP net income between $0.05 to $0.10 per diluted share on revenue of $324.8 million to $330.8 million. Analysts polled by FactSet expect $0.09 and $320.5 million, respectively.

data.title: Sienna Senior Living Brief: Says Continues to grow with $81 million of "High Quality" acquisitions in Ontario
Southwest Gas Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.62 a Share, Payable June 2 to Holders of Record on May 15
Empire State Realty Trust Q4 Core FFO Falls, Revenue Increases
data.description:
Empire State Realty Trust reported Q4 core funds from operations late Wednesday of $0.24 per diluted share, down from $0.25 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.22.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was $197.6 million, up from $192.9 million a year earlier.

Four analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $188.8 million.

The real estate investment trust said it expects core FFO of $0.86 to $0.89 per diluted share. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect $0.92.

data.title: Rollins Prices $500 Million Debt Offering
data.description:
Rollins said late Wednesday it has priced its $500 million offering of 5.25% senior notes due 2035.

The offering is expected to close Feb. 24, the company said. It plans to use the proceeds for debt repayment and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Australia Shares Poised to Slip Amid Earnings Deluge — Market Talk
data.description: Australia's S&P/ASX 200 looks set to slip again at the open as investors parse earnings from some of the country's largest companies. ASX futures are down by almost 0.5% ahead of Thursday's session, suggesting that the benchmark index could add to the 1.6% decline it has compiled across three losses so far this week. Ahead of the open, iron-ore miner Rio Tinto posted a 15% rise in annual profit. Retail conglomerate Wesfarmers raised its 1H profit 2.9%, telecommunications provider Telstra increased 1H earnings 5.8%, and pallet giant Brambles raised its annual cashflow guidance. In the U.S., the DJIA edged 0.2% higher, the S&P 500 rose 0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.1%. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Host Hotels & Resorts falls on dour 2025 FFO forecast
data.description:
** Shares of Host Hotels & Resorts fall 2.7% to $16.61 in extended trading

** HST expects full-year adjusted funds from operations between $1.82 and $1.91 per share, compared with estimate of $1.97 apiece

** The company reported fourth-quarter adjusted FFO of 44 cents per share, compared with analysts' estimate of 40 cents per share

** In 2024, the shares of the company fell nearly 10%

data.title: Sonic Healthcare Posts Higher Earnings in Fiscal H1 as Revenue Rises
data.description:
Sonic Healthcare reported Thursday fiscal half-year statutory earnings of AU$0.492 per diluted share, up from AU$0.426 per diluted share a year earlier.

Analysts polled by Visible Alpha expected diluted EPS of AU$0.49.

Revenue for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$4.67 billion, up from AU$4.31 billion a year earlier. Analysts surveyed by Visible Alpha expected total revenue of AU$4.65 billion.

The company said it is on track to achieve its fiscal-year earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization guidance of AU$1.7 billion to AU$1.75 billion on a constant currency basis.

The board declared a dividend of AU$0.44 per share, up from AU$0.43 in the previously reported period, payable on March 20 to shareholders of record as of March 6.

About Fidelity Investments Canada ULC

At Fidelity Investments Canada, our mission is to build a better future for our clients. Our diversified business serves financial advisors, wealth management firms, employers, institutions and individuals. As the marketplace evolves, we are constantly innovating and offering our clients choice of investment and wealth management products, services and technological solutions all backed by the global strength and scale of Fidelity. With assets under management of $292 billion (as at February 18, 2025), Fidelity Investments Canada is privately held and committed to helping our diverse clients meet their goals over the long term. Fidelity funds are available through financial advisors and online trading platforms.

Read a fund's prospectus and consult your financial advisor before investing. Exchange-traded funds are not guaranteed; their values change frequently and past performance may not be repeated. Commissions, management fees, brokerage fees and expenses may all be associated with investments in exchange-traded funds and investors may experience a gain or loss.

Find us on social media @FidelityCanada

https://www.fidelity.ca

data.title: Nutrien Brief: Board also approved the purchase of up to 5% of Nutrien's issued and outstanding common shares
Pan American Silver Swings to a Fourth-quarter Profit as Revenue Rises; 2025 Production Outlook Issued
data.description:
Pan American Silver slipped 1.2% in after-hour New York trading after the company on Wednesday said swung to a fourth-quarter profit on higher revenue.

The company's adjusted earnings, excluding most one-time items, stood at US$126.9 million, or US$0.35 per share, compared with a loss of US$16.3 million, or US$0.04 per share, in the year-ago quarter. FactSet expected US$0.36 per share.

Revenue rose to US$815.1 million from US$669.6 million. FactSet projected US$794.6 million.

For the quarter ended Dec. 31, silver production was 6-million ounces, and gold production was 224.2-thousand ounces.

For 2025, the company expects silver production to be between 20-million and 21-million ounces, and gold production to be between 735,000 and 800,000 ounces. It forecast all-in sustaining cost (AISC) for the Silver segment is between $16.25 and $18.25 per ounce and for the Gold segment between $1,525 and $1,625 per ounce.

The company declared a cash dividend of $0.10 per common share, payable on or about March 14 to holders of record as of March 3.

The company's shares were last seen down US$0.29 to US$24.03 after hours. They closed down $0.42 to $34.59 on the Toronto Stock Exchange.
